top_signals.title: Iran warns U.S. of Hormuz ‘red line,’ says it will retaliate if Trump carries out threats
top_signals.summary: Trump warned that the U.S. will target Iranian infrastructure next week if negotiations do not resume.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Vitol Said to Be in Talks to Sell Shale Unit to Carnelian, EnCap
top_signals.summary: Vitol Group is in late stage talks to sell its shale oil venture VTX Energy Partners LLC to a consortium of two private equity buyers - Carnelian Energy Capital and EnCap Investments, a deal that would mark a scaling back for the trader in US upstream oil and gas production.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: The United States produced more crude oil than any other country in 2025
top_signals.summary: The United States remained the world's largest crude oil producer in 2025, according to our International Energy Statistics database, extending a streak that began in 2018 when the United States overtook Russia to become the world's leading producer.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: MOLLE, PALS, and the Empty Loops of America’s Civil-Military Divide
top_signals.summary:
Editor’s note: This is the third article in a limited series celebrating American defense technologies born from wartime and their effects on broader national security, politics, and society. This series will run for several weeks to commemorate America’s 250th anniversary, and winners will be selected by a reader vote undertaken through our newsletter later this summer. Prior installments can be found at the Arsenal of Innovation page. In the half-century since the Gates Commission established the all-volunteer force — and especially over the last 25 years — the relationship between the American soldier and the state has changed profoundly. top_signals.title: U.S. exports of crude oil and petroleum products reached record in April
top_signals.summary: U.S. petroleum exports reached a record in April as disruptions to international crude oil and refined product flows through the Strait of Hormuz increased global demand for U.S. exports. Exports increased to 13.6 million barrels per day (b/d) in April, 15% more than the previous record set in March.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Sweetgreen Shares Slide to Three-Month Low on Cyclosporiasis Outbreak Fears
top_signals.summary: Sweetgreen Inc. has lost more than a quarter of its market value in just four days as investors dump the stock due to fears about outbreak of cyclosporiasis — an intestinal infection caused by parasites in raw produce and water that has sickened thousands across the US.

top_signals.title: The 250-year history of U.S. energy consumption
top_signals.summary: Over the 250-year history of our nation, energy consumption has evolved from wood use in the 18th and 19th centuries to today's use of modern renewable, hydrocarbon, and nuclear technology. In 2025, total energy used in the United States was 96 quadrillion British thermal units (quads), up 2% from 2024, but below 2007's record 99 quads. Petroleum was the most-used energy source last year, followed closely by natural gas. Use of renewable, coal, and nuclear energy each made up about 9% of total energy use.
